title: So why did they bother putting Lilliths soul in Rei
display_title: So why did they bother putting Lilliths soul in Rei
wikitext:
[]: So, why did they bother putting Lillith's soul in Rei?             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sat Apr 23, 2011 6:02 pm Title says it all: I've thought about it and can't think why they did it. I'm sure it's obvious but whatever the reason is it has slipped my mind for a few weeks now.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 23, 2011 6:13 pm They wanted a controlled third impact that would do what they wanted. (Gendo: joing everyone together, Seele: Merge everyone into a higher being) To control it, they had to control lillith (see Fuyu in EoE, after the preparations are complete: "...It all depends on Lillith now") To control Lillith, they placed her soul in a human body with ears so they could talk to her and tell her to execute the TI they wanted. ...Also, they could not just pull a soul out of their asses and in NGE, artificial beings like Rei or the EVAS apparently don't have a soul to begin with. And it was probably in the prophecy.

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Sat Apr 23, 2011 6:43 pm I bit of a left-field theory here, but I think the creation of Rei may have been an accident that Gendo ended up using to how own advantage. Could've easily missed some details, so I'll list the ones which I think are relevant: After Yui was absorbed into Unit 01, the staff attempted to forcibly extract her. This is alluded to by Ritsuko in episode 20, when they are attempting to do the same thing with Shinji. She said the experiment failed the first time (after all, Yui is still in there).... but did it have an unexpected side-effect? Was Rei "born" as a result of the extraction? This assumes that Unit 01 was still attached to Lilith and was still in the process of being made, and I think there's an image floating around somewhere to support that. Fuyutsuki states that Rei is "The product of my despair, but at the same time the vessel of your [addressing Gendo] hopes." The latter makes perfect sense in the series, but why would Fuyutsuki create Rei? Sure, he misses Yui.... but he has almost no interaction with Rei at all during the course of the series. This implies that Gendo wasn't the one who created Rei even though he is the one using her, not to mention Professor Fuyutsuki is confirmed to be a scientific expert whereas Gendo is not (all his technical knowledge, on the rare occassions when he displays that he has some, can be attributed to osmosis from simply being around the labs all the time; note that this is referring to his technical knowledge, he's clearly a master of politics). Since Fuyutsuki was present at the time when Yui was absorbed, I'm guessing he was the one who headed the extraction attempt. It was a failure in the sense that it didn't get Yui out.... but at the same time it accidentally created something which looked a lot like her but was the same age (physically) as her son. That thing was Rei. The Rei clones could have a very simple source - they've fallen off of Lilith. Note all the little legs sprouting off of her mid-section before the Lance is pulled out of her. I can't recall exactly when, but one might have fallen off during the series. Just a hypothesis on my part. In summation: Rei wasn't intended to be "born", but when trying to extract Yui she was an unforseen byproduct. Gendo saw how she could be useful immediately(?), and began plans to use her as a means to get Yui back once and for all. EDIT: Found the relevant image to the first point (Eva-01 attached to Lilith). Image as seen in Episode 23'. Image cleared up a bit to show only Eva-01 attached to Lilith. The odd bit is that I didn't realize when the image is from - the "Reiquarium" scene . Lilith is important to Rei.... so why specifically show Lilith still attached to Unit 01 as a semi-transparent image over the Rei clones? This actually implies that there is a stronger connection to Rei's "birth" and the contact experiment with Yui than I realized.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Apr 23, 2011 6:59 pm Since Rei was implicitly created as byproduct of the salvage operation of Unit 01, her existence is basically an accident. HIP doesn't require the existence of Rei, merely that of Lilith (or even an effective substitute for Lilith, such as Unit 01). The plan to control Lilith was rooted in the use of the Lancea Longini, not anything to do with Rei. This is why Seele were so pissed that Gendou got rid of the Lance - Gendou, at that point, didn't need the Lance to control Lilith, since he was relying on his bond with Rei to coerce her into doing what he wanted, and Seele knew that Gendou was putting himself in the position to play out his own Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sat Apr 23, 2011 8:06 pm Kendrix wrote: They wanted a controlled third impact that would do what they wanted. (Gendo: joing everyone together, Seele: Merge everyone into a higher being) To control it, they had to control lillith (see Fuyu in EoE, after the preparations are complete: "...It all depends on Lillith now") To control Lillith, they placed her soul in a human body with ears so they could talk to her and tell her to execute the TI they wanted. ...Also, they could not just pull a soul out of their asses and in NGE, artificial beings like Rei or the EVAS apparently don't have a soul to begin with. And it was probably in the prophecy.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sat Apr 23, 2011 8:19 pm Rei being created was mostly likely an accident.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sat Apr 23, 2011 8:56 pm Azathoth wrote: Since Rei was implicitly created as byproduct of the salvage operation of Unit 01, her existence is basically an accident. HIP doesn't require the existence of Rei, merely that of Lilith (or even an effective substitute for Lilith, such as Unit 01). The plan to control Lilith was rooted in the use of the Lancea Longini, not anything to do with Rei. This is why Seele were so pissed that Gendou got rid of the Lance - Gendou, at that point, didn't need the Lance to control Lilith, since he was relying on his bond with Rei to coerce her into doing what he wanted, and Seele knew that Gendou was putting himself in the position to play out his own Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Apr 24, 2011 1:37 am The prevailing theory is that Rei was born after Yui's Contact Experiment when Naoko and others attempted to extract Yui. Lilith's soul was sucked into the process, mixed with Yui's LCL/DNA, and out popped Rei 1. After that it seems that Nerv developed a way to split Lilith's soul between the subsequent Rei clones and Unit-00, which lends insight into why Rei is always so confused about her identity. She's essentially been separated from the rest of her self. As for why they split the soul after Rei I's death, it was likely to control her.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sun Apr 24, 2011 5:44 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: The prevailing theory is that Rei was born after Yui's Contact Experiment when Naoko and others attempted to extract Yui. Lilith's soul was sucked into the process, mixed with Yui's LCL/DNA, and out popped Rei 1. After that it seems that Nerv developed a way to split Lilith's soul between the subsequent Rei clones and Unit-00, which lends insight into why Rei is always so confused about her identity. She's essentially been separated from the rest of her self. As for why they split the soul after Rei I's death, it was likely to control her.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 24, 2011 7:40 am child of Lilith wrote: Rei being created was mostly likely an accident.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 24, 2011 11:11 am Stryker wrote: Just like everyone else in the series! Just kidding. And anyways, that isn't factual; Asuka was a test tube child, she was being made.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Apr 24, 2011 11:12 am Stryker wrote: Just kidding. And anyways, that isn't factual; Asuka was a test tube child, she was being made.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sun Apr 24, 2011 11:19 am Asuka's a test-tube baby in the manga!? I should go read that already... I already bought Volume 12 after seeing it in a shop and skimming through to see SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 24, 2011 12:32 pm Lousy tangent, guys. By the way, Ritsuko says that Naoko had programmed the original salvage operation, so it need not have been the salvage operation itself that created Rei. If Rei had been made out of the salvage operation, Naoko, who designed the program, would have known. The sight of Rei 1 wouldn't have creeped her out with her resemblance to Yui. Fuyutsuki likely thought of using Naoko's work as a basis for extracting Lilith's soul, something that he managed without letting Naoko know. Perhaps this is when Gendo and Fuyutsuki created their grand plans to get Yui back, and factored in Lilith's soul as a critical part of the equation. Turning Lilith into a pseudo-Lilin would have made her controllable, as opposed to letting her soul stay dormant in her original body or store it in a jar somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by katastroff » Sun Apr 24, 2011 1:04 pm I'm pretty sure it was part of Gendo's plan for instrumentality. To initiate third impact, you need Adam to come in contact with Lillith, and so Gendo chose to have Adam implanted in his hand, and Rei physically be Lillith herself. As we see in EoE, his plan backfires since he didn't expect Rei to act on her own will. Imagine your car suddenly starts driving itself!

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Apr 24, 2011 2:39 pm UrsusArctos wrote: By the way, Ritsuko says that Naoko had programmed the original salvage operation, so it need not have been the salvage operation itself that created Rei. If Rei had been made out of the salvage operation, Naoko, who designed the program, would have known. The sight of Rei 1 wouldn't have creeped her out with her resemblance to Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:17 pm UrsusArctos wrote: By the way, Ritsuko says that Naoko had programmed the original salvage operation, so it need not have been the salvage operation itself that created Rei. If Rei had been made out of the salvage operation, Naoko, who designed the program, would have known. The sight of Rei 1 wouldn't have creeped her out with her resemblance to Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:29 pm Not even "her work". Episode 20 wrote: TEXT: Operation Plan Type EP2 Salvage Work Procedural Outline (and Type LP3 Complementation Procedure) IBUKI Salvage Plan Outline... Only you could have created all this in just one month. RITSUKO: Unfortunately, I didn't come up with the idea. It's data from experiments completed ten years ago. IBUKI This kind of thing happened during the Eva's development? RITSUKO: It was before I worked here. It seems my mother was here, but I only know the data. IBUKI: And how did it come out back then? RITSUKO: I heard they failed.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Apr 25, 2011 2:32 am liquidus118 wrote: How could Lillith's soul get sucked into the process when Unit 01 was a clone and therefore without a soul?
